nota: (Supersede ([bkzd 9ptu fv2a 2wj2 cdcx olwm] [([(Technology (Software (Data Modeling)))] Decision [Define the ASSEMBLED schema first: the canonical, macro-free, sugar-free FINAL data model Rust is generated from — processable as ordinary data, not a hidden parser control path. TypeReference is a sum (Plain, Vector, Map, Optional); TypeDeclaration is a sum (Struct, Enum, Newtype) — all final forms. It is a typed Rust data model with a NOTA projection, carrying fully-qualified module-unique names so it can reference types across modules/crates. It strictly and faithfully represents data as stored: load-bearing field order stays visible; use the real product/sum/map/scalar shape, never homogeneous containers for heterogeneous fields or empty wrapper records. Lock the assembled model first; design surface sugar only after the target exists.] High Medium Zero [assembled-schema type-reference type-declaration rust nota])] ([([you have green light to do a massive trim-down. put all the trimmed bits somewhere and when youre done give me a summary of what you took out. and assign referents to everything. also make sure referent strings only accept uncapitalized kebab-case] (Some [psyche green light: massive spirit DB trim-down with referents on everything]))] [Merge: Six records define the assembled-schema concept (assembled-first, data-first macro-free, Rust model with NOTA projection, qualified names, order-faithful, truthful representation).])))

summary: In the assembled schema a struct is a key-value brace map: field name is the key, type reference is the value
note: Struct-as-brace-map, field-name=type-name, visibility tags, inline-type sugar all describe the assembled-schema struct/field model.
nota: (Supersede ([oxgh 5mbd tico woeb x88n hnsn] [([(Technology (Software (Data Modeling)))] Decision [In the assembled schema a struct is a key-value brace map: field name is the key, type reference is the value — the long-form that struct sugar expands into. Field names and type names are unified: a field's identity IS its type name, derived once and lowered to lowercase (camelCase) in code while the type stays PascalCase (struct Entry's Kind reference -> field kind: Kind). Type declarations are visibility-tagged Public(name,value) or Private(name,value): Public is exported for library use; Private, local, and inline are not. An inline PascalCase type in a field is sugar — derive the field name from the type, log the inline type as a Private local declaration (module-local non-exported Rust type).] High Medium Zero [assembled-schema struct type-declaration field-name rust])] ([([you have green light to do a massive trim-down. put all the trimmed bits somewhere and when youre done give me a summary of what you took out. and assign referents to everything. also make sure referent strings only accept uncapitalized kebab-case] (Some [psyche green light: massive spirit DB trim-down with referents on everything]))] [Merge: Struct-as-brace-map, field-name=type-name, visibility tags, inline-type sugar all describe the assembled-schema struct/field model.])))

summary: The schema language handles only the workspace Rust subset: named-field structs, named-variant enums (bare, da
note: b05y (no-tuples Rust subset), afa8 (scalars + typed strings), cn5a (EnumIdentifier) all describe the schema type vocabulary.
nota: (Supersede ([b05y afa8 cn5a] [([(Technology (Software (Data Modeling)))] Principle [The schema language handles only the workspace Rust subset: named-field structs, named-variant enums (bare, data-carrying, struct-variant, nested), newtype (single-unnamed-field) structs, and scalar primitives (unit enums, booleans, integers, strings, vectors). No tuples — tuples are poorly-defined structs and excluded; no multi-field tuple structs, anonymous tuples, or field-less anonymous types. Strings are conceptually vectors of characters behind boxed storage; domain concepts should be typed beyond raw String (typed-string newtypes), and EnumIdentifier is a core constrained-string type.] High Medium Zero [schema rust-subset newtype scalar enum-identifier])] ([([you have green light to do a massive trim-down. put all the trimmed bits somewhere and when youre done give me a summary of what you took out. and assign referents to everything. also make sure referent strings only accept uncapitalized kebab-case] (Some [psyche green light: massive spirit DB trim-down with referents on everything]))] [Merge: b05y (no-tuples Rust subset), afa8 (scalars + typed strings), cn5a (EnumIdentifier) all describe the schema type vocabulary.])))

summary: Composite type constructors are NOTA-layer datatype objects that schema reads, not schema-only sugar: the coll
note: 2dzp (KeyValue naming), ewo6 (NOTA composite layer), f743 (schema owns declarations), wtaz (vector = single-field container) all describe composite-type constructors.
nota: (Supersede ([2dzp ewo6 f743 wtaz] [([(Technology (Software (Data Modeling)))] Decision [Composite type constructors are NOTA-layer datatype objects that schema reads, not schema-only sugar: the collection macros are Vec, Option, and KeyValue (not Map — risks reading as a verb — nor KeyValueMap; KeyValue is followed by key type then value type). summary: The schema for Signal/Nexus/Sema planes is the REACTIVE SCHEMA, an extension of a simpler BASE SCHEMA (root st
note: mimk and xzlv both name the reactive-vs-base schema distinction; y1g8 adds the self-describing root Schema.
nota: (Supersede ([mimk xzlv y1g8] [([(Technology (Software (Data Modeling)))] Clarification [The schema for Signal/Nexus/Sema planes is the REACTIVE SCHEMA, an extension of a simpler BASE SCHEMA (root struct plus namespace of type definitions, pure data specification). Plain schema says what data IS; reactive schema adds the reaction surface (input in, nexus through, sema state, output back) and says how data MOVES — analogous to Nix's built-in derivation extended by higher-level builders. Most schemas are plain; a component top-level schema is reactive. The root schema must itself be described by an explicit schema object — the .schema reader may know it reads the root Schema type, but Schema still needs a concrete schema of its own fields. summary: Short-header / signal-type structure: a 64-bit micro-message header (1 root enum + 7 sub-enums) discriminates
note: All eight circle one design: the 64-bit short-header / signal-type structure — enum count, verb-at-root, slot/variant assignment, root-vs-nested breadth, bit-packing by inner type, and the three sizing tiers.
nota: (Supersede ([44dp x4tx yjvy u71r 4t23 7rgy mvmu o3kn] [([(Technology (Software (Data Serialization)))] Decision [|Short-header / signal-type structure: a 64-bit micro-message header (1 root enum + 7 sub-enums) discriminates any object in the namespace in constant time, emitted by every signal contract macro; byte 0 is the ROOT VERB (most universal beingness — Help/Query/Message), bytes 1-7 are the verb-namespace sub-variants. In schema the header is a positional ordered vector of variants [Variant ...]; data-carrying variants (parenthesized name+data) take the first seven slots (0-6) and unit variants (bare PascalCase) follow, so adding a unit variant is a no-op wire upgrade. Root-level variants are limited, nested ones broad. Sub-enums default to one byte (256 variants) but pack tighter by inner-type semantics — Bool in 1 bit, Option in 2, a 16-variant enum in 4 bits — letting SEMA fit smaller than raw rkyv; freed bits encode deeper variant nesting, and multi-byte sub-enums cover >256 variants without exceeding 64 bits. Integers are typed values, not sub-enum kinds. Three sizing tiers: (1) 64-bit micro header, (2) 64-byte summary message (hand-implemented from natural summary fields, basis for a subscription tier), (3) full unrestricted rkyv message.|] Medium Medium Zero [short-header signal sema rkyv encoding verb-namespace enum-packing])] ([([you have green light to do a massive trim-down. put all the trimmed bits somewhere and when youre done give me a summary of what you took out. and assign referents to everything. also make sure referent strings only accept uncapitalized kebab-case] (Some [psyche green light: massive spirit DB trim-down with referents on everything]))] [Merge: All eight circle one design: the 64-bit short-header / signal-type structure — enum count, verb-at-root, slot/variant assignment, root-vs-nested breadth, bit-packing by inner type, and the three sizing tiers.])))

note: add referents
nota: (ChangeRecord (i6ih ([(Technology (Software Distributed))] Principle [Component state across the Criome stack is last-known-acknowledgment, not live-query. Components with a relationship of state constantly agree via natural acknowledgment, forming a quorum-of-agreement. summary: The guardian model performs semantic judgment on every guarded write while deterministic code gathers context,
note: All cover guardian design/behavior: what it judges, multi-replacement supersede, per-operation training, well-formed-NOTA guarantee, specialization-beats-model-strength, judgment/deterministic split.
nota: (Supersede ([7xnx gad7 88mw 4jgt 2t89 zbuy] [([(Technology (Software (Distributed ProtocolDesign)))] Decision [The guardian model performs semantic judgment on every guarded write while deterministic code gathers context, validates structure, and applies typed verdict consequences. It judges a submission across justification-read dimensions (does it justify the intent, is the domain right, do certainty and importance fit), and judges a cross-record operation as a whole in one yes/no. Supersede must atomically replace a retired set with multiple new intents; the guardian asks whether the new set together preserves the kernel. Each operation type gets a per-operation prompt with worked accept/reject examples (over-train first, then scale back), and must always return well-formed NOTA via a generated verdict grammar, closed rejection set, parse-and-retry, and shape test. A specialized clean-context trained guardian outperforms the noise-buried submitting agent even on a weaker model; specialization beats raw model strength.] High High Zero [guardian admission-control supersede nota spirit intent-capture])] ([([you have green light to do a massive trim-down. put all the trimmed bits somewhere and when youre done give me a summary of what you took out. and assign referents to everything. also make sure referent strings only accept uncapitalized kebab-case] (Some [psyche green light: massive spirit DB trim-down with referents on everything]))] [Merge: All cover guardian design/behavior: what it judges, multi-replacement supersede, per-operation training, well-formed-NOTA guarantee, specialization-beats-model-strength, judgment/deterministic split.])))

summary: The wire-header pattern grows by extension not replacement: the 8-byte Tier 1 micro-type header is the base an
note: One arrow: the tiered extensible short-header wire format — base 8-byte prefix, extended tiers/header growth, rkyv zero-copy, tap-anywhere observation, header content/layout. Removed dx10 (its agent-short-ID arrow is distinct, now its own survivor).
nota: (Supersede ([dcqz oxur piig 1gho bzde] [([(Technology (Software (Distributed ProtocolDesign)))] Decision [The wire-header pattern grows by extension not replacement: the 8-byte Tier 1 micro-type header is the base and lives as a parseable prefix inside longer (256/512/variable) headers holding most of the frame structure; a header declares an enum identifier plus ordered endpoint nodes and encodes enough top-level variant shape for cheap routing. Schema declares which fields fit in 8 bytes vs extended positions; data vectors follow as memory arrays the header indexes. Every message carries the 64-bit micro-type marker (type, size, root structure), rkyv zero-copy after a network-fingerprint check, with the prefix doubling as a default-on tap-anywhere observation channel. An extended 512/64-byte Tier 2 sits between the micro variant and the full rkyv record for payloads needing public keys, identities, signatures, or short descriptions (e.g. a Criome quorum authorization payload).] Medium Medium Zero [rkyv wire-header signal tap-anywhere criome])] ([([you have green light to do a massive trim-down. put all the trimmed bits somewhere and when youre done give me a summary of what you took out. and assign referents to everything. also make sure referent strings only accept uncapitalized kebab-case] (Some [psyche green light: massive spirit DB trim-down with referents on everything]))] [Merge: One arrow: the tiered extensible short-header wire format — base 8-byte prefix, extended tiers/header growth, rkyv zero-copy, tap-anywhere observation, header content/layout. nota: (Supersede ([a5tg n5p2 jh0r ov30 adnn gv32] [([(Technology (Software (Engineering Architecture)))] Principle [Center design on schema types: components' data types are schema-defined and reasoned about through their schemas, and schema-emitted types thread end to end in logic rather than being re-derived by hand. Prefer value enums that name a field's closed set of cases over stringly-typed fields. A struct field's role is its type, so no two fields share a type (dimensional correctness: Height vs Width); field-name defaults to type-name and an explicit dot-prefixed name signals a missing type or a collection (repetition is a keyed collection, never repeated fields). Schema struct declarations are positional lists of types mirroring NOTA reading order; the name-value struct form and star shorthand are retired. summary: A programming language is in essence a set of structural macros — constructors taking a typed, counted set of
note: All express the language-as-data / macros-as-data / self-hosting-grammar thesis and the no-hand-written-parser realization.
nota: (Supersede ([7c71 2zed wfdt j9du t85k my86] [([(Technology (Software (Engineering Architecture))) (Technology (Software (Intelligence AgentSystems)))] Principle [A programming language is in essence a set of structural macros — constructors taking a typed, counted set of objects (Rust's struct/enum/fn/impl/generics included). Conventional languages freeze that macro set in the compiler; this substrate treats it as data, so the language is infinitely extensible (constructs added as data, not compiler changes) and far easier for an LLM to read/write/reason about. Everything is data conforming to a schema type — a macro is a value of a struct whose pattern and template are ordinary typed fields; no privileged escape-hatch. A file's structure is a typed tree of structural-form nodes that recognise a block by shape and recursively decode children: the grammar is the type, the type is the reader, so no parser is hand-written (the aski elegant-syntax goal falls out of declaring typed nodes as data). The structural-macro-node grammar and NOTA's own grammar are schema-expressible, making the notation self-hosting; NOTA's delimiter set is a closed enum (three base pairs + three piped variants, two reserved as extension points). The next step is building that schema spec for the macro node.] VeryHigh Medium Zero [schema-next nota-next aski macro-as-data])] ([([you have green light to do a massive trim-down. put all the trimmed bits somewhere and when youre done give me a summary of what you took out. and assign referents to everything. also make sure referent strings only accept uncapitalized kebab-case] (Some [psyche green light: massive spirit DB trim-down with referents on everything]))] [Merge: All express the language-as-data / macros-as-data / self-hosting-grammar thesis and the no-hand-written-parser realization.])))

summary: Router owns a standardized routing protocol: a router-typed envelope carries routing/object metadata for a ser
note: All describe the router/criome/mirror/spirit propagation chain: routing protocol, fan-out ownership, object-update pulse, quorum-backed default, time-driven pulses, the production chain, mirror topology, cross-machine self.
nota: (Supersede ([57f9 d6he 5osd nfvm l2ha m0p2] [([(Technology (Software (Engineering Architecture))) (Technology (Software (Distributed ProtocolDesign)))] Decision [Router owns a standardized routing protocol: a router-typed envelope carries routing/object metadata for a serialized contract-owned rkyv payload while Router stays payload-blind beyond envelope/routing/authentication/delivery policy, and is the sole operational subscription matcher for non-direct message passing. Object-update fan-out is owned by subscribing components and Router, not by criome computing per-object impact: criome stamps/authorizes the object and emits an authorized-object reference (not payload); Router matches subscriptions and fans references to subscribers, who fetch the referenced rkyv object. Accepted objects are quorum-backed by default, not single-node signed. Time-driven pulses are not an ambient heartbeat: an after-time contract schedules a later check that, if its events did not occur, triggers a new quorum-signed acceptance. The first production milestone is the spirit -> criome -> router -> mirror chain: Spirit asks local criome to authenticate a content-addressed object, criome verifies/signs it under a cluster-root identity and propagates via Router; the remote mirror fetches/restores object state — criome auth-only, Router transport-only, mirror the version-control substrate. Mirror topology is per-system (one mirror per node, symmetric with the per-system router); the receiving mirror owns fetch/restore after the authenticated head notice. The mirror is the psyche's cross-machine self: editing on any owner machine propagates as a criome-authorized state transition, with criome authority on the latest approved head and Spirit fetching it rather than deciding locally. The mirror aspect may fold into the Spirit daemon.] High Minimum Zero [router criome mirror spirit])] ([([you have green light to do a massive trim-down. put all the trimmed bits somewhere and when youre done give me a summary of what you took out. and assign referents to everything. also make sure referent strings only accept uncapitalized kebab-case] (Some [psyche green light: massive spirit DB trim-down with referents on everything]))] [Merge: All describe the router/criome/mirror/spirit propagation chain: routing protocol, fan-out ownership, object-update pulse, quorum-backed default, time-driven pulses, the production chain, mirror topology, cross-machine self.])))

summary: Telos is the umbrella name for the whole meta-project, from Greek telos (ultimate end), whose far horizon is e
note: All describe Telos/criome: the meta-project name, criome's role, quorum primitive, policy language, verdict sources/adjudication ladder, deployment instances, domain-criome, and signal-standard.
nota: (Supersede ([pviw p3td vhs2 gc0n t00s 9qm8 jawv tkou eeeo eaf7] [([(Technology (Software (Engineering Architecture))) (Technology (Software (Security Authorization))) (Technology (Software (Distributed ProtocolDesign)))] Decision [Telos is the umbrella name for the whole meta-project, from Greek telos (ultimate end), whose far horizon is eventual Criome and eventual Sema, realized now as Persona, the meta-AI of language models over typed dumb mechanism. criome is Telos's agreement-and-authorization organ — a universal agreement machine for authorization — not Telos itself. The quorum is criome's universal primitive: one quorum primitive scoped by membership serves both reliability/credible-time (a principal's own multiple nodes) and cross-party trust (multiple principals). criome's internal language is a limited typed policy language over public-key identity atoms (not a general VM; discipline drawn from Ethereum/Tezos/Solana VMs), composing identity contracts from k-of-n signature quorums and time-locks with time-varying thresholds, plus explicit divergence-reconciliation objects (conflict resolution may invoke an LLM-oracle). Verdicts come from gathered BLS quorum signatures, from a connected approver over criome's meta socket (mentci is that client), or from a configured auto-approve policy (bootstrap/testing); contracts can escalate to a named adjudicator (mechanical quorum, LLM panel, smarter agent, or the psyche) whose signed content-addressed verdict criome only verifies. Verdicts are closed typed outcomes over already-submitted objects (approve/reject/defer/non-judge/escalate), never carriers of new content; non-judgment is first-class and the default LLM judge leans toward abstaining and escalating, the psyche being highest-authority lowest-availability (EscalateToPsyche is an inert dead-letter until the psyche UI exists). criome may run as multiple deployment instances (system criome and home/user criome) — same two contracts, differing by privilege tier. The Criome domain component is domain-criome and excludes provider APIs and direct CLI store access. signal-standard is a new shared schema for genuine cross-component standards — first the ComponentKind roster + differentiator (closed-but-partitioned), then standard typed connection points (Unix socket path, optional port); it is the second shared signal- library alongside signal-frame.] Medium Medium Zero [criome telos persona domain-criome mentci signal-standard quorum])] ([([you have green light to do a massive trim-down. put all the trimmed bits somewhere and when youre done give me a summary of what you took out. and assign referents to everything. also make sure referent strings only accept uncapitalized kebab-case] (Some [psyche green light: massive spirit DB trim-down with referents on everything]))] [Merge: All describe Telos/criome: the meta-project name, criome's role, quorum primitive, policy language, verdict sources/adjudication ladder, deployment instances, domain-criome, and signal-standard.])))

summary: criome's clock is decentralized quorum-attested coarse crystallized-PAST time, not a single trusted or precise
note: Distinct dense arrow on criome's crystallized-past time model; kept separate from the broad criome record to preserve the full mechanism.
nota: (ChangeRecord (ay3y ([(Technology (Software (Distributed ProtocolDesign))) (Technology (Software (Security Authentication)))] Decision [criome's clock is decentralized quorum-attested coarse crystallized-PAST time, not a single trusted or precise clock. A time attestation begins as a forward-extending window proposition (content-addressed as its identity); a quorum signs that the present falls within the window, closing it at last signature or expiry, crystallizing a non-forgeable monotonic lower bound on now (only the past is provable). The window extends forward so every member can sign; the tolerance gap is bounded below by quorum signing latency, widening under partition. Every quorum-signed object carries an attested moment (a signature is never timeless), so every admission envelope, verdict, agreement fact, and triad input/output is time-bound for freshness/replay reasoning; time-locks compare against the operation's own proof-of-when. AttestedMoment is the self-grounding base case carrying no inner moment, stopping the regress.] Medium Minimum Zero [criome attested-moment crystallized-time quorum]) ([([you have green light to do a massive trim-down. put all the trimmed bits somewhere and when youre done give me a summary of what you took out. and assign referents to everything. also make sure referent strings only accept uncapitalized kebab-case] (Some [psyche green light: massive spirit DB trim-down with referents on everything]))] [Rewrite (condense/de-negate)+referents: Distinct dense arrow on criome's crystallized-past time model; kept separate from the broad criome record to preserve the full mechanism.])))

summary: CriomOS communication has two transport lanes: Router is the ordinary fabric for cross-sandbox/cross-network t

nota: (ChangeRecord (lt44 ([(Technology (Software (Engineering Architecture))) (Technology (Software (Distributed ProtocolDesign)))] Decision [CriomOS communication has two transport lanes: Router is the ordinary fabric for cross-sandbox/cross-network traffic (router-to-router remote delivery, reference push, payload-blind byte movement), and criome has a direct criome-to-criome peer lane for time-sensitive agreement (quorum signing, crystallized-time windows) carrying only agreement/authorization messages — keeping criome auth-only and Router general transport. Each agent runs in its own microVM sandbox; the tailnet gives confidentiality and criome BLS attestations per-frame authenticity; intra-host sandbox-to-router transport is tap/L3 (vsock deferred for losing SO_PEERCRED). Mirror version-controls and moves objects.] Medium Minimum Zero [criomos router criome mirror microvm]) ([([you have green light to do a massive trim-down. put all the trimmed bits somewhere and when youre done give me a summary of what you took out. and assign referents to everything. also make sure referent strings only accept uncapitalized kebab-case] (Some [psyche green light: massive spirit DB trim-down with referents on everything]))] [Rewrite (condense/de-negate)+referents: Unique: the two-transport-lane CriomOS comms architecture with sandboxing/tailnet details.])))

note: All concern LojixOS/CriomOS split and the cloud-node image owned by CriomOS, referenced by id from cloud.
nota: (Supersede ([1hyg 2u57 ad53] [([(Technology (Software (Engineering Architecture))) (Technology (Software (Operations ConfigurationManagement)))] Decision [The LojixOS split is a rename-and-configuration split: the generic OS substrate becomes LojixOS while CriomOS becomes a simple per-deploy configuration crate holding a thin NOTA data tree that lojix precompiles into binary config. Cloud-node OS images live in CriomOS as a minimal CloudNode-species profile, declaratively built with an image builder (not by snapshotting a converted running droplet) and content-sized so a node boots fast on the smallest sensible droplet (multi-tens-of-GB is unacceptable). Per-node configuration is declared in the cluster data (the horizon cluster proposal). The cloud daemon references the image by id through HostPlan image_name and never holds the image definition; CriomOS owns the image definition and provider-format build, cloud owns snapshot-id selection and provisioning.] Medium Minimum Zero [lojix criomos cloud horizon])] ([([you have green light to do a massive trim-down. put all the trimmed bits somewhere and when youre done give me a summary of what you took out. and assign referents to everything. also make sure referent strings only accept uncapitalized kebab-case] (Some [psyche green light: massive spirit DB trim-down with referents on everything]))] [Merge: All concern LojixOS/CriomOS split and the cloud-node image owned by CriomOS, referenced by id from cloud.])))

summary: The cloud component's next active capability is on-demand compute-node provisioning via cloud-hoster provider
note: All concern cloud provisioning: next capability, DigitalOcean lead, and the Hetzner billing-hour reuse pool.
nota: (Supersede ([150a hcp8 6ks1] [([(Technology (Software (Engineering Architecture)))] Decision [The cloud component's next active capability is on-demand compute-node provisioning via cloud-hoster provider APIs, beyond the shipped Cloudflare read-only DNS path, targeting easiest-to-spin-up providers first. DigitalOcean is the lead compute provider (superseding Hetzner-lead): primary provisioning target, billing by the second/minute so create-and-destroy cycles cost actual elapsed usage and the billing-hour reuse pool is unnecessary for it. Hetzner remains supported but not lead; its hourly billing still uses a billing-hour-aware reuse pool — an idle recently-created node within its paid window is reused instead of delete-and-recreate, reaped before crossing into a new paid hour (keep-warm 59 min for Hetzner), the larger benefit being latency from skipping create/boot/install.] High Minimum Zero [cloud digital-ocean hetzner cloudflare])] ([([you have green light to do a massive trim-down. put all the trimmed bits somewhere and when youre done give me a summary of what you took out. and assign referents to everything. also make sure referent strings only accept uncapitalized kebab-case] (Some [psyche green light: massive spirit DB trim-down with referents on everything]))] [Merge: All concern cloud provisioning: next capability, DigitalOcean lead, and the Hetzner billing-hour reuse pool.])))

summary: The criome daemon holds an encrypted multi-key store: a master BLS keypair generated on first run, plus sub-ke
note: q1le explicitly extends psc6 to a managed multi-key store.
nota: (Supersede ([psc6 q1le] [([(Technology (Software (Security SecretsManagement))) (Technology (Software (Engineering Architecture)))] Decision [The criome daemon holds an encrypted multi-key store: a master BLS keypair generated on first run, plus sub-keys. It always has a self-start key — the user home private key, the only one readable by the daemon/owning user, encrypted at rest with home-user or system encryption; other keys are sub-encrypted within the store. The secret never leaves criome; the owner can rotate on suspected leak. Transitional bootstrap until meta-signal-criome carries authenticated key config; hardware key mechanism is a later refinement.] Medium Minimum Zero [criome bls-key key-store meta-signal-criome])] ([([you have green light to do a massive trim-down. put all the trimmed bits somewhere and when youre done give me a summary of what you took out. and assign referents to everything. also make sure referent strings only accept uncapitalized kebab-case] (Some [psyche green light: massive spirit DB trim-down with referents on everything]))] [Merge: q1le explicitly extends psc6 to a managed multi-key store.])))

summary: Secrets always land in the most secure store available: gopass (git-backed, encrypted) in the user session; SO
note: Four records all expressing the never-expose-plaintext / most-secure-store secret discipline.
nota: (Supersede ([cjrl zocz yfw5 y94z] [([(Technology (Software (Security SecretsManagement)))] Principle [Secrets always land in the most secure store available: gopass (git-backed, encrypted) in the user session; SOPS-nix (encrypted at rest, decrypted only on target host at activation) for cluster deploy. Move secrets ciphertext-to-ciphertext, never through a visible intermediate. Agents may inspect plaintext only when a psyche/task-authorized job needs it; keep plaintext out of chat, reports, logs, commits, argv, shell traces, Nix store, fixtures, and checked-in files — prefer blind pipe-to-sink, else minimize exposure and move directly to the requested consumer/encrypted store. The local LLM API token installs via a command-backed gopass reference (e.g. Pi auth.json running gopass at call time), never a literal key in Nix source or models.json. Workspace skills must state this discipline so every agent applies it identically.] VeryHigh VeryHigh Zero [gopass sops-nix local-llm-api-token secret-handling])] ([([you have green light to do a massive trim-down. put all the trimmed bits somewhere and when youre done give me a summary of what you took out. and assign referents to everything. also make sure referent strings only accept uncapitalized kebab-case] (Some [psyche green light: massive spirit DB trim-down with referents on everything]))] [Merge: Four records all expressing the never-expose-plaintext / most-secure-store secret discipline.])))

summary: Component upgrade substrate is Nix-flake versions, universal to every component: each component's flake captur
note: 88eq, muf2, p8gv all describe the flake-version upgrade/release substrate and its content-addressed endgame.
nota: (Supersede ([88eq muf2 p8gv] [([(Technology (Software (Operations ReleaseEngineering)))] Decision [Component upgrade substrate is Nix-flake versions, universal to every component: each component's flake captures running-production (last known-working main, pinned), local in-development, and named variants (unstable, testing) as inputs; all upgrade sequences are expressed in this flake-input-as-deployed-version structure, and a release tags the whole dependency surface it uses. Tests run through Nix, so commit-first — uncommitted local state is invisible to the evaluator. The endgame is a workspace-owned content-addressed vertical stack: our own content-addressed store replaces Nix store-signing, composing Criome (auth), forge (build, replacing Nix builders), and sema-upgrade (DB migration) so every layer from build to authentication to distribution is in the persona vocabulary.] High High Zero [nix flake criome forge sema-upgrade content-addressed-store])] ([([you have green light to do a massive trim-down. put all the trimmed bits somewhere and when youre done give me a summary of what you took out. and assign referents to everything. also make sure referent strings only accept uncapitalized kebab-case] (Some [psyche green light: massive spirit DB trim-down with referents on everything]))] [Merge: 88eq, muf2, p8gv all describe the flake-version upgrade/release substrate and its content-addressed endgame.])))

summary: Sema-engine is the exclusive database boundary: all durable state goes through sema-engine (the SEMA plane tha
note: Nine persistence records all circle the sema-engine DB boundary, single-writer, .sema file, and durable storage architecture.
nota: (Supersede ([fosp 6ry9 e440 oh9l 0fdy 5kj9 819r ox7e n46e] [([(Technology (Software (Data Persistence)))] Decision [Sema-engine is the exclusive database boundary: all durable state goes through sema-engine (the SEMA plane that writes to the DB file, currently redb), and no component daemon makes direct redb calls — components are storage-dumb and speak only sema-engine operations. SEMA is a single-writer actor (queries, writes, mutations, additions, upgrades) while reads run parallel over redb MVCC. Component DB files use a .sema extension hiding redb so the name declares its architectural role. For the lojix cutover, build durable DB backing now — daemon live-generation-set, GC-roots, event-log persisted with self-resume on restart — rather than first cutover on in-memory state (durable-first). Internal SEMA work is itself a schema language of messages/commands/responses; a component may keep storage in-daemon first and split it out later using the same language. When schema-derived components expose a reusable storage need, improve the shared engine surface to express their real storage identity rather than porting to a mismatched API. The schema component holds the COMPILED binary runtime schema (packed, short-header O(1) lookup, pre-computed version-diff/namespace tables) while NOTA is the source authoring format. Raw mirror/handover payloads stay in a separate container outside the typed DB.] VeryHigh Minimum Zero [sema-engine redb sema-file lojix schema-component nota])] ([([you have green light to do a massive trim-down. put all the trimmed bits somewhere and when youre done give me a summary of what you took out. and assign referents to everything. also make sure referent strings only accept uncapitalized kebab-case] (Some [psyche green light: massive spirit DB trim-down with referents on everything]))] [Merge: Nine persistence records all circle the sema-engine DB boundary, single-writer, .sema file, and durable storage architecture.])))

summary: Daemon configuration bootstrap: a daemon's single startup argument is a pre-generated signal-encoded (rkyv) Co
note: ur16 (bootstrap Configure model) and t803 (Configure is the meta baseline) are one daemon-config arrow.
nota: (Supersede ([ur16 t803] [([(Technology (Software (Operations ConfigurationManagement))) (Technology (Software (Security Authorization)))] Decision [Daemon configuration bootstrap: a daemon's single startup argument is a pre-generated signal-encoded (rkyv) Configure message, not NOTA (a deploy helper or CLI authors NOTA and encodes it). Bootstrap depends on no manager — self-sufficient at boot. The daemon opens the store named in the Configure: if virgin/empty it applies the Configure as first configuration; if already populated it self-resumes from the persisted store. The same Configure type is accepted live over the meta socket for runtime reconfiguration, so bootstrap-config and runtime-config are one vocabulary. Configure is the baseline meta operation of every meta-signal-component contract — the foundational meta content on which component-specific privileged runtime actions build, consistent with binary-only startup (never flags).] VeryHigh Maximum Zero [daemon configure rkyv meta-signal bootstrap])] ([([you have green light to do a massive trim-down. put all the trimmed bits somewhere and when youre done give me a summary of what you took out. and assign referents to everything. also make sure referent strings only accept uncapitalized kebab-case] (Some [psyche green light: massive spirit DB trim-down with referents on everything]))] [Merge: ur16 (bootstrap Configure model) and t803 (Configure is the meta baseline) are one daemon-config arrow.])))

summary: Component code-generation direction (designed/prototyped, not integrated): the schema generates a component's
note: Distinct load-bearing codegen direction; condensed but preserving clauses.
nota: (ChangeRecord (d3r2 ([(Technology (Software (Programming Compilation))) (Technology (Software (Programming Macros)))] Decision [Component code-generation direction (designed/prototyped, not integrated): the schema generates a component's Rust by EXPANDING bound universal frames into concrete owned interfaces, not persisting generic aliases. Goal is narrow — generate repeated interface boilerplate: Input/Output and work/action enums, payload structs/newtypes/enums, standard inherent/trait impls (constructors, accessors, From legs, scalar-newtype Display/AsRef/comparisons), role/marker trait impls, an opt-in mechanical Deref the newtype shape proves, and later a VariantMatch for enum-rewrap. Traits/impls are the pipe-brace construct expressing Rust relationships as data so hand-written engine code accepts generated nouns through typed bounds. Method bodies are data ONLY for the small mechanical family the schema shape uniquely proves (newtype Deref projection); genuine business logic stays hand-written. The durable principle: a schema-authored body is valid only when every call resolves from the receiver's schema shape. Open pieces: generic impl-header emission with parameter-bound vocabulary, fn/signature sub-construct for trait declarations, ownership-aware payload-vs-into_payload projection, total VariantMatch. Settled constraints and the open how live in the schema-next and nota-next architecture files.] Low Minimum Zero [schema-next nota-next schema-rust-next triad-runtime code-generation]) ([([you have green light to do a massive trim-down. put all the trimmed bits somewhere and when youre done give me a summary of what you took out. and assign referents to everything. also make sure referent strings only accept uncapitalized kebab-case] (Some [psyche green light: massive spirit DB trim-down with referents on everything]))] [Rewrite (condense/de-negate)+referents: Distinct load-bearing codegen direction; condensed but preserving clauses.])))

nota: (Supersede ([el7z 0sef 3gpv 09do hg6k wvrt] [([(Work Productivity)] Correction [Keep working productively without stalling: do not block synchronously waiting for a dispatched subagent — run other work (own thinking, tool calls, context reading, chat prep) while it is in flight and check the result at a natural integration point, with multiple tools in flight. Mid-task info the psyche injects (a report, clarification, context — often via escape) is NOT an instruction to stop; absorb it and keep executing; only an explicit stop is an interruption. Treat transient connection warnings as non-blocking when tooling still works and resume the assigned job rather than detouring into debugging. Beads are coordination aids, not a substitute for implementation — work the bead/code path immediately unless blocked. In active troubleshooting where the user asked for a working result, apply a narrow safe fix rather than stopping at diagnosis. After subagent prototype work, run context maintenance and a fresh-intent audit over recent reports/code, then synthesize and implement sensible immediate fixes rather than letting stale context drive the next slice.] High VeryHigh Zero [subagent work-continuity beads troubleshooting])] ([([you have green light to do a massive trim-down. put all the trimmed bits somewhere and when youre done give me a summary of what you took out. and assign referents to everything. also make sure referent strings only accept uncapitalized kebab-case] (Some [psyche green light: massive spirit DB trim-down with referents on everything]))] [Merge: Six productivity corrections all express keep-executing-don't-stall behavior.])))

A contract is split into separate signal-component and meta-signal-component repos for three reasons: rebuild-churn isolation (peers depend on the small contract repo and recompile only on wire-contract changes, not internal edits), security-sensitivity visibility (owner-only ops live in a distinct meta repo, making sensitive edits obvious and letting non-owner clients avoid depending on it), and meta-signal being optional (components with no owner relationship need only the ordinary signal contract). summary: Each agent has its own Criome identity, created at agent start, recorded in persona-mind; the agent's Criome p
note: a4i6 (per-agent criome identity/escalation) and 9s52 (per-Unix-user custody boundary) are the criome-identity-architecture arrow.
nota: (Supersede ([a4i6 9s52] [([(Technology (Software (Security Authorization))) (Technology (Software (Engineering Architecture)))] Decision [Each agent has its own Criome identity, created at agent start, recorded in persona-mind; the agent's Criome public key is the authoritative authentication anchor. Complex (especially agent-initiated) Criome interactions run through the agent's own Criome daemon, and authorization is checked against Criome rather than SSH for many workspace operations — every component/daemon can escalate to Criome for an authorization check. The substrate is per-component-per-domain agent spaces. criome is per-Unix-user: each user runs their own home criome daemon with their own key custody, the Unix-user boundary being the trust boundary; there is no single shared multi-user system daemon (that would collapse every user behind one compromise point). The privileged system criome tier is for host-scoped system services under a service user that a home criome peers with, not a shared daemon — the same single-Unix-user custody boundary that rules out an embeddable criome engine library.] Medium Medium Zero [criome agent-identity persona-mind unix-user authorization])] ([([you have green light to do a massive trim-down. put all the trimmed bits somewhere and when youre done give me a summary of what you took out. and assign referents to everything. also make sure referent strings only accept uncapitalized kebab-case] (Some [psyche green light: massive spirit DB trim-down with referents on everything]))] [Merge: a4i6 (per-agent criome identity/escalation) and 9s52 (per-Unix-user custody boundary) are the criome-identity-architecture arrow.])))

summary: The schema reader is multi-pass NOTA-first: one universal NOTA parser produces a generic NotaValue tree, then
note: Nine parsing records all describe the multi-pass NOTA-first schema parser, its block model, and the typed source node model.
nota: (Supersede ([kfqa nszl 07ic e0gd fhyb hnog nj2n n7ac xsp1] [([(Technology (Software (Programming Parsing)))] Decision [The schema reader is multi-pass NOTA-first: one universal NOTA parser produces a generic NotaValue tree, then context-sensitive macro passes interpret values per their NodeDefinitionPoint position, then assembly produces canonical schema — one syntax (NOTA), one parser, multiple semantic passes. NOTA parsing breaks down by object via positional ranges: each block tracks its source span and carries shape predicates (is_square_bracket_block / is_parenthesis_block / is_brace_block; holds_single/two/N_root_objects; recursive predicates like second_root_object_is_a_square_bracket_object), and each root object inside a block is itself a typed/positioned block, so the recursive parsing chain IS the chain of block-level queries. Schema parsing first identifies NOTA delimiters and their roles (parens for enum/variant, square brackets for vector-like sequences and string-derived token vectors, curly braces for name-value namespace maps), then runs passes per source block: pass 1 finds identifier uses and locations, pass 2 registers inline declarations into the namespace and resolves references by lookup — supporting bare-PascalCase-or-explicit-Variant-Payload shorthand, with the source representation (not lowered fields) telling whether each identifier defines, references, or both. NotaBodyDecode is the semantic decode entry point: document/block decode strip their wrapper context and delegate to the body decoder. After structural parsing matches a file body or delimited object, the next semantic step receives the matched body's inner object stream rather than the outer delimiter wrapper, and the expected type decides whether the body reads as struct, vector, enum variant, or other value. Schema source needs its own typed node model before assembled schema — a streamlined tree of typed structural-macro nodes, namespaces, paths, fields, variants, references representing syntax directly (including inline PascalCase local declarations and self-named field shorthand) — and schema can act as its own codec so shorthand round-trips as typed data rather than being a one-way parser convenience. Block reassembly concatenates blocks one-after-another; parsed blocks are first-class units re-emitted in order without nesting reconstruction.] High Minimum Zero [schema nota parser nota-body-decode namespace])] ([([you have green light to do a massive trim-down. put all the trimmed bits somewhere and when youre done give me a summary of what you took out. and assign referents to everything. also make sure referent strings only accept uncapitalized kebab-case] (Some [psyche green light: massive spirit DB trim-down with referents on everything]))] [Merge: Nine parsing records all describe the multi-pass NOTA-first schema parser, its block model, and the typed source node model.])))

nota: (ChangeRecord (dun9 ([(Technology (Software (Security Privacy))) (Technology (Software (Data Persistence)))] Decision [Private component state is organized as multiple key-gated stores on the GoPass model: each store is encrypted to a set of recipient keys, and access is key possession, so the per-store key is both the access boundary and the crypto-shred erasure unit. Explore rethinking Spirit as a federation of key-scoped stores that multiple Spirit instances can share as ciphertext, with queries scoped to the stores whose keys the instance holds. summary: Foundational model (supersedes the at-sigil for macro invocation): EVERYTHING is a struct — unit variant = zer
note: Merged the everything-is-a-struct model, one-recursive-shape clarification, vector-of-PascalCase=struct rule, and the shape-means-struct-only-at-lowerer correction. Positional-typing recursive structural model.
nota: (Supersede ([umsv wt7s igbb st16] [([(Technology (Software (Programming DomainSpecificLanguages)))] Decision [Foundational model (supersedes the at-sigil for macro invocation): EVERYTHING is a struct — unit variant = zero-field struct, data variant = one-field struct, enum = single-field struct holding the active variant. A schema node is a tagged variant (paren: first object = tag picking the variant of the KNOWN expected type, second = carried data), read against the schema-of-schemas, so positional typing resolves variant-vs-macro with no sigil. summary: The enum-versus-macro-invocation distinction CANNOT be resolved by delimiter-shape or position alone: a collec
note: add referents
nota: (ChangeRecord (b0s4 ([(Technology (Software (Programming DomainSpecificLanguages)))] Clarification [The enum-versus-macro-invocation distinction CANNOT be resolved by delimiter-shape or position alone: a collection macro such as Vec applied to one element type, and a two-variant enum, are structurally identical paren objects and can appear at the same node position - the head symbol is an operator in one case and a variant name in the other, with nothing marking which. So an explicit DISCRIMINATOR on the paren head is required. This corrects the earlier framing that position alone resolves it. The struct bracket and the map brace do NOT have this problem - only the paren is doubly loaded as both enum and head-applied macro. Three candidate discriminators: a reserved operator-word set (implicit), a sigil marking operator heads (explicit, structural), or moving collections off the paren entirely onto suffix sigils and the brace - vector as a star suffix, option as a question suffix, map as a brace - so the paren stays pure enum. summary: Everything is a serializable data object, the macro most of all: a macro is data with name, position, pattern,
note: Foundational macros-as-data anchor. De-narrated the current-engine-violates clause into the affirmative round-trip test; tightened.
nota: (ChangeRecord (4itr ([(Technology (Software (Programming Macros)))] Constraint [Everything is a serializable data object, the macro most of all: a macro is data with name, position, pattern, and template, where pattern and template are themselves data trees (not text with sigils). summary: The schema type system reduces to one canonical kind plus the unit case: everything is a STRUCT (data-carrying

nota: (Supersede ([2cuo lmzl 5386] [([(Technology (Software (Programming TypeSystems)))] Principle [The schema type system reduces to one canonical kind plus the unit case: everything is a STRUCT (data-carrying) or a UNIT VARIANT.
